name: Poori (Whole Wheat Indian Bread)
recipeInstructions:
1. Make 8 pooris.
2. In a medium bowl, combine the whole wheat flour and self-rising flour. Mix in the buttermilk. Knead the dough on a lightly floured surface for 2 minutes. Cover and let rest for 15 minutes.
3. Divide the dough into 8 pieces. Roll one piece into a smooth ball (keep the remainder covered with a towel). Dust generously with whole wheat flour and roll into a 5-inch-diameter circle, dusting with flour to prevent sticking. Cover. Repeat with the remaining dough.
4. Heat oil in a wok or medium saucepan to a depth of 3 inches. Heat until it reaches 375 degrees.
5. Add one dough round to the hot oil. The dough will sink to the bottom. When the dough starts to rise, gently press in the center with the back of a slotted spoon until the bread puffs. Turn and cook until light brown, about 20 seconds. Remove from the oil using a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels.
6. Keep the pooris warm while cooking the remaining dough. Place them on a platter and serve immediately.
